subtopic: Uploaded KAUTILYA sample smart note with mind map

content.issueStory: The nationalist movement did not begin with mass struggle. It first learned to speak. The Moderates built the vocabulary of rights; the Extremists gave it urgency.
content.coreConcept: Moderates believed in constitutional agitation, petitions, persuasion, and gradual reforms. Extremists emphasized self-reliance, boycott, swadeshi, passive resistance, and more assertive nationalism. The Surat Split reflected deeper differences in method, pace, and political temperament.
dimensions: Early Nationalism: INC formation; Political associations; Economic critique; Constitutional agitation
Moderates: Prayer-petition-protest; Faith in British liberalism; Drain theory; Civil liberties
Extremists: Swaraj; Swadeshi; Boycott; National education; Passive resistance
Surat Split: Moderate-extremist tension; Leadership conflict; Methods dispute; Temporary organizational weakness
Swadeshi Movement: Partition of Bengal; Boycott; Indigenous enterprise; Cultural nationalism
PYQ Traps: Moderate methods vs extremist methods; Timeline confusion; Leaders and slogans; INC session facts
Mains Hooks: Political awakening; Economic nationalism; Mass base expansion; Continuity with Gandhian era
content.constitutionalLink: Historical anchor: link the theme to chronology, actors, doctrine and UPSC match-the-pair traps.
content.dataReport:
“Moderates prepared the mind; Extremists prepared the will.”
Use in questions on political methods, economic nationalism, and ideological evolution of Congress.
content.caseStudy:
Write a 150-word comparison: “Moderates and Extremists differed in method, not in love for India.”

---
argumentsFor: “Moderates prepared the mind; Extremists prepared the will.”
Use in questions on political methods, economic nationalism, and ideological evolution of Congress.
argumentsAgainst: Dadabhai Naoroji is linked with Drain Theory and “Poverty and Un-British Rule in India.”
1906 Calcutta session accepted Swaraj as the goal under Dadabhai Naoroji’s presidentship.
Gokhale was a Moderate, not an Extremist.
Lala Lajpat Rai had nationalist, social, and institutional contributions, including Servants of the People Society.
Sri Aurobindo moved from revolutionary politics to spirituality later.

story: The nationalist movement did not begin with mass struggle. It first learned to speak. The Moderates built the vocabulary of rights; the Extremists gave it urgency.
core_concept: Moderates believed in constitutional agitation, petitions, persuasion, and gradual reforms. Extremists emphasized self-reliance, boycott, swadeshi, passive resistance, and more assertive nationalism. The Surat Split reflected deeper differences in method, pace, and political temperament.
mnemonic:
Moderates = “3P”
- Petition
- Prayer
- Protest

Extremists = “4S”
- Swaraj
- Swadeshi
- Boycott/Struggle
- Self-reliance

Leaders:
- Moderates: Dadabhai Naoroji, Gopal Krishna Gokhale, Pherozeshah Mehta, Surendranath Banerjea
- Extremists: Tilak, Bipin Chandra Pal, Lala Lajpat Rai, Aurobindo
